get.description:
Indicates if Zitadel is running.
 It respondes as soon as Zitadel started

post.description:
Returns a list of Zitadel instances

get.description:
Returns the detail of an instance

 Deprecated: Use [GetInstance](apis/resources/instance_service_v2/zitadel-instance-v-2-instance-service-get-instance.api.mdx) instead to get the details of the instance in context

put.description:
Updates name of an existing instance

post.description:
Adds a domain to an instance

Removes the domain of an instance

post.description: Sets the primary domain of an instance

Returns all stored read models of Zitadel
 views are used for search optimisation and optimise request latencies
 they represent the delta of the event happend on the objects

Truncates the delta of the change stream
 be carefull with this function because Zitadel has to
 recompute the deltas after they got cleared.
 Search requests will return wrong results until all deltas are recomputed

Returns event descriptions which cannot be processed.
 It's possible that some events need some retries.
 For example if the SMTP-API wasn't able to send an email at the first time

delete.description:
Deletes the event from failed events view.
 the event is not removed from the change stream
 This call is usefull if the system was able to process the event later.
 e.g. if the second try of sending an email was successful. the first try produced a
 failed event. You can find out if it worked on the `failure_count`

put.description:
Sets quota configuration properties
 Creates a new quota if it doesn't exist for the specified unit

unit.title: unit
unit.description: the unit a quota should be imposed on

from.title: from
from.description: the starting time from which the current quota period is calculated from. This is relevant for querying the current usage.

limit.title: limit
limit.description: whether Zitadel should block further usage when the configured amount is used

notifications.title: notifications
notifications.description: the handlers, Zitadel executes when certain quota percentages are reached

post.description:
Creates a new quota
 Returns an error if the quota already exists for the specified unit
 Deprecated: use SetQuota instead

userResourceOwner.title: user_resource_owner
userResourceOwner.description: The organization the user belong to.

percent.title: percent
percent.description: The percentage relative to the quotas amount on which the call_url should be called.

repeat.title: repeat
repeat.description: If true, the call_url is called each time a factor of percentage is reached.

callUrl.title: call_url
callUrl.description: The URL, which is called with HTTP method POST and a JSON payload with the properties "unit", "id" (notification id), "callURL", "periodStart", "threshold" and "usage".

sequence.title: sequence
sequence.description:
sequence represents the order of events. It's always counting

 on read: the sequence of the last event reduced by the projection

 on manipulation: the timestamp of the event(s) added by the manipulation

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description:
creation_date is the timestamp where the first operation on the object was made

 on read: the timestamp of the first event of the object

 on create: the timestamp of the event(s) added by the manipulation

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description:
change_date is the timestamp when the object was changed

 on read: the timestamp of the last event reduced by the projection

 on manipulation: the

resourceOwner.title: resource_owner
resourceOwner.description: resource_owner is the organization an object belongs to

connect-protocol-version.title: Connect-Protocol-Version
connect-protocol-version.description: Define the version of the Connect protocol

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of [google.rpc.Code][google.rpc.Code].

message.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the [google.rpc.Status.details][google.rpc.Status.details] field, or localized by the client.

google.protobuf.Any.description: Contains an arbitrary serialized message along with a @type that describes the type of the serialized message.
